subject: Mathematics
source: National Curriculum Framework for School Education (NCF-SE), Section 3.4.1.2

CG-1.label: Number Sense and Patterns
CG-1.description: Understands numbers and sets of numbers (whole numbers, fractions, integers, rational numbers, and real numbers), looks for patterns, and appreciates relationships between numbers

competency_codes.C-1.1: Develops a sense for and an ability to manipulate (e.g., read, write, form, compare, estimate, and apply operations) and name (in words) large whole numbers of up to 20 digits, and expresses them in scientific notation using exponents and powers
competency_codes.C-1.2: Discovers, identifies, and explores patterns in numbers and describes rules for their formation (e.g., multiples of 7, powers of 3, prime numbers), and explains relations between different sets of numbers (whole numbers, fractions, integers, rational numbers, and real numbers)
competency_codes.C-1.3: Learns about the inclusion of zero and negative quantities as numbers, and the arithmetic operations on them, as given by Brahmagupta
competency_codes.C-1.4: Explores and understands sets of numbers, such as whole numbers, fractions, integers, rational numbers, and real numbers, and their properties, and visualises them on the number line
competency_codes.C-1.5: Explores the idea of percentage and applies it to solve problems
competency_codes.C-1.6: Explores and applies fractions (both as ratios and in decimal form) in daily-life situations

CG-2.label: Algebra and Equations
CG-2.description: Understands the concepts of variable, constant, coefficient, expression, and (one-variable) equation, and uses these concepts to solve meaningful daily-life problems with procedural fluency

competency_codes.C-2.1: Understands equality between numerical expressions and learns to check arithmetical equations
competency_codes.C-2.2: Extends the representation of a number in the form of a variable or an algebraic expression using a variable
competency_codes.C-2.3: Forms algebraic expressions using variables, coefficients, and constants and manipulates them through basic operations
competency_codes.C-2.4: Poses and solves linear equations to find the value of an unknown, including to solve puzzles and word problems
competency_codes.C-2.5: Develops own methods to solve puzzles and problems using algebraic thinking

CG-3.label: Geometry: Shapes and Properties
CG-3.description: Understands, formulates, and applies properties and theorems regarding simple geometric shapes (2D and 3D)

competency_codes.C-3.1: Describes, classifies, and understands relationships among different types of two- and three-dimensional shapes using their defining properties/attributes
competency_codes.C-3.2: Outlines the properties of lines, angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons and applies them to solve related problems
competency_codes.C-3.3: Identifies attributes of three-dimensional shapes (cubes, parallelepipeds, cylinders, cones), works hands-on with material to construct these shapes, and also uses two-dimensional representations of three-dimensional objects to visualise and solve problems
competency_codes.C-3.4: Draws and constructs geometric shapes, such as lines, parallel lines, perpendicular lines, angles, and simple triangles, with specified properties using a compass and straightedge
competency_codes.C-3.5: Understands congruence and similarity as it applies to geometric shapes and identifies similar and congruent triangles

CG-4.label: Measurement: Perimeter, Area and Theorems
CG-4.description: Develops understanding of perimeter and area for 2D shapes and uses them to solve day-to-day life problems

competency_codes.C-4.1: Discovers, understands, and uses formulae to determine the area of a square, triangle, parallelogram, and trapezium and develops strategies to find the areas of composite 2D shapes
competency_codes.C-4.2: Learns the Baudhayana-Pythagoras theorem on the lengths of the sides of a right-angled triangle, and discovers a geometric proof using areas of squares erected on the sides of the triangle, and other related geometric constructions from the Sulba-Sutras
competency_codes.C-4.3: Constructs various designs (using tiling) on a plane surface using different 2D shapes and appreciates their appearances in art in India and around the world
competency_codes.C-4.4: Develops familiarity with the notion of fractal and identifies and appreciates the appearances of fractals in nature and art in India and around the world

CG-5.label: Data Handling and Statistics
CG-5.description: Collects, organises, represents (graphically and in tables), and interprets data/information from daily-life experiences

competency_codes.C-5.1: Collects, organises, and interprets the data using measures of central tendencies such as average/mean, mode, and median
competency_codes.C-5.2: Selects, creates, and uses appropriate graphical representations (e.g., pictographs, bar graphs, histograms, line graphs, and pie charts) of data to make interpretations

CG-6.label: Mathematical Reasoning and Communication
CG-6.description: Develops mathematical thinking and the ability to communicate mathematical ideas logically and precisely

competency_codes.C-6.1: Applies both inductive and deductive logic to formulate definitions and conjectures, evaluate and produce convincing arguments/proofs to turn these definitions and conjectures into theorems or correct statements, particularly in the areas of algebra, elementary number theory, and geometry

CG-7.label: Mathematical Creativity and Problem Posing
CG-7.description: Engages with puzzles and mathematical problems and develops own creative methods and strategies to solve them

competency_codes.C-7.1: Demonstrates creativity in discovering one's own solutions to puzzles and other problems, and appreciates the work of others in finding their own, possibly different, solutions
competency_codes.C-7.2: Engages in and appreciates the artistry and aesthetics of puzzle-making and puzzle-solving

CG-8.label: Computational Thinking
CG-8.description: Develops basic skills and capacities of computational thinking, namely, decomposition, pattern recognition, data representation, generalisation, abstraction, and algorithms in order to solve problems where such techniques of computational thinking are effective

competency_codes.C-8.1: Approaches problems using programmatic thinking techniques such as iteration, symbolic representation, and logical operations and reformulates problems into series of ordered steps (i.e., algorithmic thinking)
competency_codes.C-8.2: Learns systematic counting and listing, systematic reasoning about counts and iterative patterns, and multiple data representations; learns to devise and follow algorithms, with an eye towards understanding correctness, effectiveness, and efficiency of algorithms

CG-9.label: History and Culture of Mathematics
CG-9.description: Knows and appreciates the development of mathematical ideas over a period of time and the contributions of past and modern mathematicians from India and across the world

competency_codes.C-9.1: Recognises how concepts (like counting numbers, whole numbers, negative numbers, rational numbers, zero, concepts of algebra, geometry) evolved over a period of time in different civilisations
competency_codes.C-9.2: Knows and appreciates the contributions of specific Indian mathematicians (such as Baudhayana, Pingala, Aryabhata, Brahmagupta, Virahanka, Bhaskara, and Ramanujan)
